execution_history.reason: Portfolio analytics validate measurable improvement on risk, correlation, and return parameters per user brief, with only immaterial tail concentration in speculative tail names. No further structural changes required at this round.

Portfolio analytics validate measurable improvement on risk, correlation, and return parameters per user brief, with only immaterial tail concentration in speculative tail names. No further structural changes required at this round.

observations: Portfolio Sharpe improved (2.40, up from 2.28); Sortino and Calmar also higher. Annualized return up (0.82 vs 0.74). Beta slightly higher (1.08 from 1.03) but net exposure still within risk budget (0.98). Pairwise correlation average falls (0.20 baseline vs >0.23 previously); higher dispersion, improving diversification. Drawdown resilience, win rate, and group performance all conform to user risk/reward. Stress test reveals acceptable downside except in ultra-stress credit regimes, mainly driven by small allocations to VERO, CRDO, DGNX (idiosyncratic, high-beta but with limited portfolio impact due to small weights). Outlier tail risk on VERO is flagged for moderate future reduction, but does not materially threaten overall optimization. Overall, the portfolio shows improved alpha, lower average correlation, better risk-adjusted metrics, and stronger stress resilience vs prior. No further urgent iterations required.
